title: Delivery OS — Capability config/secret requirements (RS-DOS §57.3, additive to §31 *.capability.json)
description: ADDITIVE extension to the capability descriptor (`*.capability.json`, §31 / CAPABILITY-MANIFEST-STANDARD). It declares — as a first-class, queryable Runtime fact (not tribal knowledge rediscovered per incident) — WHICH config and WHICH secrets a capability requires, for WHICH environments. Per §57.3: `capability += { requires_config:[..], requires_secret:[..] }`, each entry `{key, data_class, env_scope, rule}`. This schema validates ONLY those two additive arrays; it does NOT re-state the rest of the capability descriptor (the §3 standard owns that, and unknown fields are forward-compatible there). The resolver (capability-config-resolver.mjs) reads these arrays and produces a WorkPackage's `config_req` set; the readiness-shadow binding (readiness-shadow.mjs) feeds that set to the I-Config oracle (i-config.mjs, §57.4) and LOGS the readiness verdict at the C9/D7/C13 gates. SHADOW in Sprint 1.3 — the verdict is reported, never blocking (the enforce-flip is gated to Sprint 2.2). The `key`/`data_class`/`env_scope`/`rule` vocabulary is the SAME as the canonical config-secret-registry (config-secret-registry.schema.json) so a capability's requirement resolves 1:1 onto a registry key.

requires_config.description: Non-secret config keys this capability needs (data_class ∈ {PUBLIC, INTERNAL, CONFIDENTIAL}). Each entry names a key that MUST resolve to a registry key. Absent/empty ⇒ the capability needs no config.

requires_secret.description: Secret keys this capability needs (data_class ∈ {PII, SECRET}); these bind to the §54.2 trust boundary (trusted-domain only; NEVER tree-resident — §30). Separated from requires_config so 'which SECRETS does this capability require' (the founder's Q2) is answerable without re-classifying. Absent/empty ⇒ the capability needs no secret.

key.description: The env var name, exactly as the capability's code reads it. MUST match a `key` in the service's config-secret-registry (the resolver flags an unresolved requirement as a registry gap).

data_class.description: The §54.1 classification. A requirement listed under requires_secret MUST be PII or SECRET; one under requires_config MUST NOT be. The resolver asserts this so the secret/config split stays honest.

env_scope.description: The environments in which THIS CAPABILITY needs the key. The resolver intersects this with the target env to decide whether the requirement applies to a given work package run.

rule.description: The expected validation_rule id (postgres-pooler-6543 | url | secret-min:N | non-empty | flag | enum:a|b | number | int-nonneg). Advisory at the capability layer: the registry's validation_rule is authoritative; the resolver warns if a capability's stated rule disagrees with the registry (a drift signal).

reason.description: OPTIONAL one-line why — what breaks if this key is MISSING/INVALID (surfaced in the readiness-shadow log for the founder audit).
